behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI report rendering. A unit test with mocked diff records can pass even if the tool invokes git without rename detection (e.g., missing rename flags), which would surface as delete+add in real repos. Integration was considered but rejected because this behavior depends on real git diff/name-status behavior from an actual repository, not only in-process module handoff. No AI/model dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit a file, rename it with git, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows 1 renamed and 0 added/0 deleted for that change set.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary-diff handling failures usually appear when parsing real git output for binary changes (or when git emits binary markers/metadata). Mocked fixtures can hide parser and process-path failures. Integration was considered but rejected because the behavior explicitly depends on real binary diff behavior from a repository/CLI execution path. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit a binary file, modify it with non-text bytes, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file is included in the correct category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The edge case depends on real repo state before first commit and the resulting git/CLI behavior (often special exit codes or missing HEAD semantics). Mocking repository state risks missing real startup/error handling failures. Integration was considered but rejected because this behavior is tied to real git repository initialization state and CLI execution outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report and successful non-error behavior.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual git history, which requires real repository evolution and CLI extraction/parsing/reporting together. Mocked history objects can miss mismatches caused by real git status/diff semantics across commits. Integration was considered but rejected because the behavior explicitly anchors truth to the repository’s real change history.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI for the target range, and assert category counts exactly match expected history totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree state transitions. In-process fakes frequently collapse these states and hide failures where CLI/git queries differ between staged and unstaged paths. Integration was considered but rejected because correctness is specifically about real git state mechanics across staging boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare identical content changes twice in a temp repo: once left unstaged, once staged; run the CLI in both states and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
